text.title: North Star Metric Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 5 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Owner field set to 'team', 'us', 'tbd', or left blank. No one can be paged when the artefact drifts.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent declined to commit a named person because the brief didn't supply one.
antipattern.fix: Reject the artefact. Require a single email / handle before persisting. If unknown, halt and ask the operator.

antipattern.symptom: Evidence array empty; field values plausible but not in the source brief / transcript / dashboard.
antipattern.root-cause: Model substituted statistical priors for real inputs.
antipattern.fix: Reject. Require ≥1 verbatim citation per non-trivial field before persisting.

antipattern.symptom: Field declared as list/dict in the schema receives a paragraph of prose instead.
antipattern.root-cause: LLM defaults to free-form prose when the prompt does not pin the structural type.
antipattern.fix: Convert to the declared structured type. If ambiguous, reject and re-emit with explicit schema reminder.

antipattern.symptom: Agent sets status='approved' on the North Star Metric Design output without any named human reviewer.
antipattern.root-cause: Agent treated 'I checked it' as approval.
antipattern.fix: Downgrade to 'ready_for_review'. Block binding actions until a named reviewer signs off.

antipattern.symptom: Last cycle's values copied verbatim even though the source-of-truth has changed.
antipattern.root-cause: Cycle-N agent did not re-pull source.
antipattern.fix: Re-pull from source-of-truth; mark carry-forward only if source_last_modified is unchanged.
